negotiation_summary: England publicly and privately proposed stability in the west in exchange for France holding (BRE H, MAR H, PAR H). England warned of a 60% chance France would act aggressively; France reciprocated with a public pledge to hold all three home centers this phase and requested England not to move into ENG, MAO, or GAS and not to attack BRE or MAR. Germany was asked for a DMZ in BUR and to pressure England; Austria was asked for a Balkan truce. No binding multilateral deal was signed, but verbal agreements: England -> France: keep BRE/MAR/PAR defensive this phase; France -> England: will not contest BEL/SPA and requests England refrain from ENG/MAO/GAS moves. Forecast artifact (England's forecast about Austria aggression, 60% aggression) was shared in negotiations and shaped promises to avoid western fighting this phase to exploit eastern volatility.
intent: I intend to fulfill the defensive commitments made: BRE H, MAR H, PAR H. I will not attempt moves into BEL, SPA, ENG, MAO, or GAS this phase. I will communicate to England and Germany that I expect their reciprocity; if England moves aggressively into BRE/MAR/MAO/GAS/ENG or coordinates supports threatening French homes, I will consider that a betrayal and respond next phase by pushing into BEL and supporting moves to contest English sea control. Note: preserve all units (no disbands), keep aggressive posture in messaging toward Austria and Turkey to encourage them to be preoccupied, but do not order offensive moves this phase. Orders to execute now: F BRE - HOLD, A PAR - HOLD, A MAR - HOLD.
goals: Expand aggressively when opportunities present, prioritizing capturing neutral or English centers once western stability breaks
Maintain and defend home centers (BRE, PAR, MAR) this phase to avoid overextension
Exploit eastern/Balkan instability next phase; coordinate opportunistic strikes if allies betray or openings appear
Preserve all units and avoid passive holds beyond this phase unless strategic

storyworld_implications.implication: England projects a 60% chance that Austria will act aggressively next phase; England used this to encourage France to stand down in the west. This implies eastern theaters (Austria/Balkans) are likely to be active, creating opportunities for France to avoid western conflict this phase and exploit distractions next phase.

forecasting_rationale: The 60% aggression forecast about Austria increases the value of a temporary western truce: holding home centers this phase conserves forces while other powers engage in the east, creating future opportunities. Given England's naval strength, immediate aggression risks losing BRE/MAR; thus fulfilling the defensive pledge preserves tempo. I prioritized a non‑aggressive stance now to force others to reveal intentions in the east and to avoid a costly two‑front fight. If the forecast proves false and England or Germany betray, the intent commits to rapid retaliation next phase.

negotiation_summary: England secured tentative non‑aggression understandings with France (France will hold BRE/MAR/PAR; England will not attack BRE/MAR/enter ENG/MAO/GAS and will keep BEL and SPA stable) — France explicitly signaled a 60% chance of acting aggressively next phase and requested the pause. England told Germany it expects KIE/HOL/DEN to be left quiet in exchange for England not pressing on Ruhr/Berlin approaches. With Russia England traded deconfliction: England will not contest STP if Russia keeps F STP/SC off NWY/SWE. England informed Austria it will not intervene in the Balkans immediately but offered to discuss support if Austria requests stabilization vs Turkey. All agreements are conditional and public warnings were issued that England will reinforce BEL/HOL/DEN/KIE if any party violates the truce.
intent: For S1915M England intends to hold western stability but remain ready to punish defection. Planned orders (intent-level, to carry forward to actual orders phase): - A LON -> ENG (prepare to threaten BRE/CHI entry if France defects) but currently will not occupy ENG unless France moves; consider as a move contingent on French aggression. - F LON (if present) or F LON equivalent: maintain Channel pressure but avoid MAO/GAS. - A HOL support A BEL hold; A BEL hold; A DEN support A KIE hold (or support A HOL->KIE if Germany contests KIE). - A EDI -> NWY (or hold if Russia keeps STP off NWY; contingency: if F STP/SC moves into NWY, A EDI supports seizure of NWY/SWE). - A LVP support A HOL->BEL (to ensure BEL stable). - A SPA hold; A POR hold (to keep Iberia stable). - A SWE hold unless Russian agreement breaks, in which case A SWE->FIN/RTC as needed. I will fulfil my declared holds (BEL, SPA, no moves into BRE/MAR/ENG/MAO/GAS) unless explicit aggression occurs. If France defects (as forecast implies 60% chance), I will execute coordinated supports from HOL and DEN into KIE/BEL and use POR/LON to apply pressure on French coastal centers.
goals: Maintain and defend current 10 supply centers; prevent opportunistic seizures of BEL and SPA this phase.
Preserve naval dominance in the Channel and Atlantic to enable future expansion into France or support operations versus Germany.
Avoid opening a costly two‑front conflict; use non‑aggression pacts to free forces to respond to eastern/Balkan volatility.
Be prepared to punish any defection promptly with coordinated supports to seize contested centers and expand toward 18 SCs.
storyworld_implications: The provided forecast (storyworld_id 'forecast_defection') indicates France is more likely (60%) to act aggressively next phase. That implies our non‑aggression understanding is fragile and should be treated as conditional. It also implies Austria may act aggressively next phase (per shared intel), increasing eastern volatility. These implications necessitate preserving flexible reserves in the north/west to respond quickly to French defection and monitoring Balkan shifts for opportunistic gains.
forecasting_rationale: Given the forecasted 60% probability of French aggression, I am treating France's promise as provisional: I therefore commit to defensive holds (BEL, SPA) but keep units positioned to respond (supports from HOL/DEN, pressure from POR/LON). The forecast increases the priority of retaining mobility and supports in the north and maintaining clear contingency orders (e.g., A EDI->NWY contingent on Russian restraint). Forecasted eastern aggression (Austria) means I will not divert significant resources to the Balkans now but will watch for openings to exploit any overstretch, preserving fleets/armies in the west for rapid redeployment.
